free_text: .ONE MW HEAVY WATER REACTOR, R1, STOCKHOLM.
THERMAL NEUTRON FLUX AT TARGET POSITION IS
GAMMA DETECTION BY GE(LI) PAIR AND ANTI-
STAFF SHAPED GE(LI) CRYSTAL, LENGTH 2.6 CM
VOLUME 3.1 CM3 AND AN ANNUAL NAI CRYSTAL AS PAIR
.ANTI-COINCIDENCE BY SAME NAI CRYSTALS FOR MEASUREMENT
.ERRORS IN RELATIVE INTENSITIES ARE ESTIMATED AS ABOUT
10 PERCENT FOR STRONG LINES AND INCREASE SUCCESSIVELY
TO ABOUT 100 PERCENT FOR WEAK LINES.
THE INTENSITY OF THE 7814 KEV LINE
FROM GROSHEV ET AL., NUCL.DATA, SECT.A,VOL.3,NO 4-6
(1967) WAS USED TO DETERMINE THE VALUE OF THE
5233.9 KEV LINE OF YB-176 AS 0.29 GAMMA S/100 NEUTRONS
WITH THE HELP OF A WEIGHTED MIXTURE OF NATURAL
.HIGH ENERGY PART, OBTAINED WITH THE PAIR SPECTROMETER.
.BASED ON THE VALUE 4.0 OF THE 1080.1 KEV GAMMA LINE
ORIGINATING FROM 1.9 HOUR BETA DECAY OF YB-177.
CONTRIBUTION FROM CAPTURE IN YB-174 IS
UNCERTAIN BECAUSE NEAR TO ANNIHILATION PEAK.
NORMALIZED TO THE VALUE 8.5 FOR THE
5724.7 KEV LINE DERIVED FROM MEASUREMENTS OF GROSHEV
ET AL., NUCL.DATA, SECT.A,VOL.5,NO 3-4 (1969), AND OF
.HIGH ENERGY PART, OBTAINED WITH THE PAIR SPECTROMETER.
.BASED ON THE SUM OF THE 214 AND 1004 KEV LINES
.LOW ENERGY PART, OBTAINED WITH THE ANTICOINCIDENCE.
